methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
First Light Fusion is a Series C nuclear energy company that has raised $100M+ to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 comparable companies—3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy—operating in advanced fission and fusion segments.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and yields a valuation of $170M, derived by applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C progression to the $100M+ total raised as a valuation proxy. The Peer-Median approach cannot be reliably applied; while 8 same-sector comparables exist, the narrow peer set lacks sufficient datapoints at matching stages and valuation tiers to support a statistically defensible median. Attempting to broaden to an all-sector median would introduce uncontrolled bias given the heterogeneity of nuclear technology maturity and commercialization timelines.

The triangulated analysis supports a median valuation of $170M, with a defensible range of $144M–$195M reflecting uncertainty bounds around the single applicable method. Key uncertainties include: the representativeness of the 1.7x multiplier for this specific capital stage within frontier nuclear; the company's path to revenue and regulatory milestones relative to named peers; and the materiality of founder track record (Hawker, Ventikos) to valuation lift. Given reliance on a single method, any refinement requires either documented comparable transaction data at equivalent stage or demonstrable progress toward clinical/commercial inflection points.
